Document:
[color=#897236][mono]──── [color=#FFA500][bold]DEFINITIONS[/bold][/color] ───────────────────────────────[/mono][/color]
[bolditalic]These definitions are used throughout the rest of Triad Sector Law and TDF SOP.[/bolditalic]
[bold]Citizen[/bold] - A sapient resident of the Triad Sector that is not a hostile actor.
\n
[bold]Hostile Actor[/bold] - Being a hostile member of a recognized hostile nation or organization.
\n
[bold]Enforcer[/bold] - Any currently active member of the TDF, including Junior Enforcers. - Venmar Station Guards are, for all intents and purposes, considered Enforcers, but with the restrictions outlined in [textlink="page one of TDF SOP" link="SOPJurisdictionalAuthority"].
\n
[bold]Chief Enforcer[/bold] - The officially appointed Commander of the TDF, carrying the rank of TDF-5. - They have additional privileges, compared to an acting Commander. - By default, the Chief Enforcer has all the privileges of the Commander.
\n
[bold]Commander[/bold] - The current Commanding Officer of the TDF. - An acting commander should be assigned as outlined by [textlink="chain of command" link="SOPJurisdictionalAuthority"], if a Chief Enforcer is not available. - An acting commander has less privileges unlike an official Chief Enforcer. - Chief Enforcer-specific privileges do not apply to an acting Commander.
\n
[bold]Factional Equipment[/bold] - Any equipment produced by a hostile organization that is of technological use to the TDF.
\n
